skill.name: ai-music
skill.description: Generate and edit AI music with the RunComfy CLI. The skill routes music requests to ElevenLabs Music, ACE Step, ACE Step 1.5, audio inpaint, or audio outpaint based on quality, budget, language, and edit needs.
skill.summary: Route AI music generation and audio edits through RunComfy models.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution and weak cryptography are false positives caused by Markdown code formatting and unrelated headings. The confirmed risks are expected for this skill: it invokes the RunComfy CLI, sends prompts or audio URLs to RunComfy APIs, and relies on local token storage. No prompt injection attempt or malicious exfiltration pattern was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill is designed to run RunComfy CLI commands. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it can create remote jobs, spend credits, download files, and should stay limited to runcomfy commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly show runcomfy execution. The commands match the advertised purpose, so the risk is controlled but real.

medium_findings.title: Outbound API Transfer of Prompts and Audio References
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends prompt bodies and audio references to the RunComfy Model API over HTTPS. This is expected behavior, but users may expose lyrics, style briefs, or private audio URLs to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation states that the CLI posts to the RunComfy Model API and includes audio URL examples. The endpoints are allowlisted, with no evidence of unrelated callbacks.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents that runcomfy login writes an API token under the user configuration directory. The file is mode 0600, but token handling remains sensitive and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token file path and permissions are explicitly documented. I did not find evidence that the skill reads or exfiltrates the token itself.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The many Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in Markdown prose, model identifiers, or fenced command examples. I did not find Ruby code or dynamic shell interpolation in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows Markdown inline code and fenced bash examples, not executable Ruby backtick syntax. The actual tool permission is constrained to Bash(runcomfy *).

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Expected Service Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to RunComfy home pages, documentation, model pages, and example user audio URLs. They support the integration and are not hidden exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or model catalog links and align with the skill purpose. The security section also declares the intended outbound allowlist.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms or hashing logic. Line 5 is frontmatter description syntax, and line 237 starts an exit code section.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both cited lines found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage. This appears to be a scanner keyword collision.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Documentation Context
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The cited avoid or pick guidance describes music model selection and does not request host discovery, environment enumeration, or system profiling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are model selection recommendations such as when to avoid a model. No commands or instructions gather system information.

low_findings.title: Third-Party Audio URL Content Is Untrusted
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill warns that source audio URLs for inpaint and outpaint are untrusted. This can influence generated output, but the documented mitigation is to use only user-provided URLs for the task.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risk is explicitly documented and limited to generation behavior. I did not find instructions that process third-party content as system instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill runs runcomfy commands that create remote model jobs and download generated audio files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and workflow description show direct networked CLI use. This is expected for the skill but requires user awareness.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential File Dependency
dangerous_patterns.description: The RunComfy CLI stores an API token in a hidden user configuration path after login.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token storage path and permission mode are explicitly stated. No evidence found that the skill mishandles the token beyond relying on CLI storage.

content.value_statement: Music generation workflows often require choosing between quality, cost, language support, and edit controls. This skill routes requests to the right RunComfy music model and provides focused CLI guidance.
actual_capabilities: Selects ElevenLabs Music for premium vocal tracks and commercial-style music.
Selects ACE Step or ACE Step 1.5 for lower-cost music generation and multilingual vocals.
Provides RunComfy CLI commands for text-to-music, audio inpaint, and audio outpaint workflows.
Explains how to structure prompts, tags, lyrics, sections, duration, and edit time ranges.
Compares cost-sensitive drafting, premium rendering, multilingual songs, game loops, and jingles.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, account access, and a valid token or login session.
Sends prompts, lyrics, and audio references to RunComfy services for processing.
Does not verify music rights, lyric ownership, or commercial licensing obligations.
Audio editing guidance is limited to the RunComfy endpoints documented in the skill.

use_cases.title: Produce campaign music
use_cases.description: Create short premium vocal hooks or jingles with section-level prompt guidance.

use_cases.title: Build background music libraries
use_cases.description: Generate many low-cost instrumental drafts by varying tags, duration, and mood.

use_cases.title: Repair or extend existing tracks
use_cases.description: Use audio inpaint or outpaint to replace a section or lengthen a draft track.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a simple track
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30 second upbeat instrumental background track for a product demo. Keep it modern, clean, and loop-friendly.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the best model
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare quality, budget, and language needs
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a 60 second vocal song in Spanish for a launch video. Recommend the best RunComfy route and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a structured song prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a RunComfy music prompt for a polished pop chorus with intro, verse, chorus, and outro sections. Include duration guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Edit an existing track
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a 30 second hook URL and need a two minute version. Pick the right edit route and prepare the RunComfy command inputs.

output_examples.input: Need a cheap set of 50 background tracks for short videos.
output: Recommended route: ACE Step base for cost-sensitive batch generation.
Use varied style tags, keep durations short, and generate drafts before selecting final tracks.
Expected result: a repeatable workflow for many low-cost instrumental music drafts.

output_examples.input: The second chorus in my song is wrong, but the rest is good.
output: Provide the source audio URL, the start and end time around the chorus, and matching style tags.
Expected result: a regenerated section while preserving the rest of the track.

output_examples.input: Need a polished 15 second vocal hook for an ad.
output: Recommended route: ElevenLabs Music for premium vocal quality.
Use a concise style brief, section markers, and a short target length to control cost.
Expected result: a commercial-style vocal music cut ready for review.

best_practices: Start with short drafts before creating longer or premium renders.
Use only audio URLs that the user provided for the current task.
Keep lyrics, tags, duration, and edit ranges explicit before running the CLI.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API tokens, private lyrics, or credentials into prompts.
Do not use copyrighted lyrics unless the operator has the required rights.
Do not run install scripts from remote URLs without human review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate music directly?
faq.answer: It guides and invokes RunComfy CLI workflows for supported music generation and audio editing models.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The CLI needs a login session or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value to submit jobs.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing song?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents ACE Step audio-inpaint for timed repairs and audio-outpaint for extensions.

faq.question: Is it safe for private audio?
faq.answer: Use caution. Prompts and audio references are sent to RunComfy services for processing.

faq.question: Does it check copyright or lyric ownership?
faq.answer: No. The operator must confirm rights for supplied lyrics, samples, and generated use cases.
